title: Fix an obvious typo
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [ ] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Was this intentional? Why keep this line instead of removing it?

This was added in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23079 , with no record of _why_ it should be necessary.

Considering that it’s been two years that no-one was broken by the typo, I am leaning towards removing it entirely, but I’m 100% ignorant of krunkit.

Cc: @slp .

can't find any occurrence of "com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validationr" on internet, so I assume it is a typo.

LGTM

should be also fixed for https://github.com/containers/krunkit/blob/main/krunkit.entitlements ?
We should test if that entitlement is even needed cc/ @jakecorrenti

The entitlement is described here https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/entitlements/com.apple.security.cs.disable-library-validation?language=objc
If krunkit does not load unsigned frameworks, this might not be needed (or maybe for development builds?)

Hey folks from Red Hat, I’d strongly recommend you spend about 350 days discussing it and run another 234 tests before even thinking about fixing this one-line obvious bug — you know, to stay consistent with your enterprise-grade open-source traditions. Just like this: https://www.reddit.com/r/redhat/comments/1pdrywk/fix_for_broken_chromepwa_icons_syntax_error_in/

The sarcasm is neither required nor appreciated. We've identified another instance of the typo in another repo, which has spawned further discussion about whether we even need the entitlement at all. These are all perfectly natural things that get discussed on pull requests, even if they seem simple.

That said, there's no reason to keep this one unmerged.
We can do a removal (if required) separately. I'm leaning towards we should keep this in for at least a release or two, though? We added this in the first place for a reason...
I'll check if we need it on the krunkit side.

> The sarcasm is neither required nor appreciated.

Yes. Just because a typo can be _eliminated_ does not mean we got the best outcome for the project and its users. It’s. quite possible we have just increased users’ risk for no benefit by merging this. And given the Podman release schedule, there is _absolutely no benefit_ to rushing.

---

> That said, there's no reason to keep this one unmerged.

FWIW, I think keeping the PR, discussion and investigation in one open PR would have been easier than having this PR merge and then either tracking separately or not tracking.

I doubt such a simple issue is worth this much discussion. The correct course of action is obvious: remove that line. I’ve been waiting for Podman 6 to be released, and clearly Spring 2026 isn’t happening, which is why I’m frustrated.

@erictrue 

Apologies for the [Podman 6 delay](https://blog.podman.io/2026/05/podman-6-release-delayed/) - we're trying our best to get it through the door, but some time crunches have come our way and we wanted to make sure some features that required breaking changes made the cut - or else we'd have to wait 2 more years to get them in. If you have any concerns, our discussions tab is always open.

Much of the Podman team are Linux engineers. MacOS is not our strong suit, and although this looks like a simple typo, with a seemingly obvious course of action, our team does not have the expertise to confirm and verify, so we tagged in some people that are familiar with that area to double check that this is the right move. As we're drawing close to Podman 6, the last thing we want to do is to accidentally remove an entitlement that prevents Podman on Mac from running smoothly.

Our goal is always to make Podman the best it can be. Please be patient with us and [communicate respectfully](https://github.com/cncf/foundation/blob/main/code-of-conduct.md#our-standards): We strive to foster open discussion and a welcoming community, and we'd expect the same from our contributors.

> I doubt such a simple issue is worth this much discussion. The correct course of action is obvious: remove that line. 

The original PR proposed a different course of action, so maybe it is not _that_ obvious.
